title: centaurea pygmaea
intro: Centaurea pygmaea, also known as the Pygmy Cornflower, is a small, annual plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections. The plant's health benefits include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, and its antimicrobial properties may help to prevent the spread of infections. Centaurea pygmaea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
